titleStmt.title: Letter from John Mangan regarding Sir John O'Connell, 3 April 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Mangan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by John Mangan as an endorsement of John Robert O'Connell for the vacant chief crown Solicitor position. Mangan (1853-1917) was the Bishop of Kerry in 1916. Mangan writes that his chief experience of O'Connell (1868-1943) was as a public man and that as such he contributed largely to the public good. Mangan believes that the south of Ireland is indebted to O'Connell for his services.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/17

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
people. +John Mangan
Bishop of Kerry. April 3rd 1916 THE PALACE,
KILLARNEY. I have experience
of Sir John R. oConnell
for some years
past both professionally
and in other respects. In every way as
far as my knowledge
goes he has been
very successful. My chief experience
of him has been
as a public man
and as such he
contributed very
largely to the public
good. In the South
of Ireland we are
very largely indebted
to him for his excellent
services to the University
College at Cork. I may say that he
has in very large
measure the respect and
confidence of the
best class of our
